---
title: "Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation Practice Implications"
description: "Practice synthesis for Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "handbook-practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Practice"
citation_key: "newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:part-1:c0001"
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch01-chapter-1-planning-and-designing-useful-evaluations:c0003"
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch05-chapter-5-performance-measurement:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

This practice synthesis draws on selected notes from *Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation* [@newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation].

## Evaluation Practice Implications

- Balance rigor, cost, timing, and likely usefulness.
- Fit the evaluation design to the program's life cycle and decision context.
- Treat performance measurement and evaluation as complementary evidence systems.
- Use stakeholder engagement to sharpen, not dilute, evaluation focus.

## Applying the Notes

Use these implications as prompts during scoping, design review, method selection, and reporting. They should be adapted to the evaluation's decision context, available evidence, and user needs.

## Links

- [Evaluation Planning and Design](section-01-planning-and-design.qmd)
- [Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations](section-02-planning-useful-evaluations.qmd)
- [Performance Measurement and Program Evaluation](section-03-performance-measurement.qmd)

## References
